extraction.one_line_summary: CICCONE attacked alone with 3km remaining on Col de Port descent after breaking away from ALAPHILIPPE, held off VINGEGAARD by 23 seconds.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE exhausted breakaway companions on Col du Grenier, enabling CICCONE to counterattack final climb without resistance.
CASTROVIEJO bridged 1:30 gap from peloton on Col du Grenier, unusually ambitious tactical commitment by INEOS domestique.
CICCONE attacked with 3km left knowing descent advantage, not waiting for summit—descending skill decisive over pure climbing.
VINGEGAARD allowed breakaway margin until Col de Port, then paced conservatively—team prioritized GC defense over stage hunt.

rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: timed solo attack perfectly with 3km remaining, used descending superiority rather than pure climbing to distance chasers
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: strong enough to drive breakaway all day but lacked finishing power on Col de Port
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: dominated GC by 2:23, comfortable enough to pace second climb rather than chase stage win
rider_signals.CASTROVIEJO Jonathan: positive: bridged 1:30 to breakaway during Col du Grenier, exceptional domestique effort though ultimately dropped
rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: neutral: took KOM points on HC Grenier but unable to follow final selection
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: secured podium with aggressive riding, clear third in GC battle
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: third on stage but GC gap to VINGEGAARD reveals pure climbing deficit
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: finished within standards post-injury, progressive improvement but still subpar compared to pre-crash form
rider_signals.PEDRERO Antonio: neutral: attacked penultimately climb aggressively but lacked legs to catch CICCONE or contest stage
